name: Postal & Community
description_short: Postal & Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution based in St. Joseph, Missouri, offering traditional banking and lending products including checking, savings, credit cards, and mortgages.
description_long: Postal & Community Credit Union operates as a traditional credit union serving members in the St. Joseph, Missouri area. The organization has been established long enough to have a routing number (301281968) and maintains both a main office and mailing address, indicating a brick-and-mortar presence with operational infrastructure. As a credit union, the institution is structured as a member-owned cooperative rather than a for-profit bank, which typically means members share in any profits through better rates and lower fees. The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning consumer banking and lending. Their product lineup includes checking and savings accounts with published rates, credit cards, auto loans, mortgages (including refinancing), and personal loans. The organization maintains traditional banking infrastructure including ATMs, though the website notes current maintenance issues requiring temporary shutdown. Members can contact the organization through multiple channels including phone (816-279-5656), fax, and email for account and loan services. Postal & Community Credit Union distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, which theoretically provides more favorable terms than traditional banks due to the member-ownership model. The organization maintains established banking relationships and regulatory compliance, as evidenced by their routing number and published disclosures. Their long-standing presence in St. Joseph and commitment to member services ("Once a Member. Always a Member") suggests institutional stability and community rootedness. Prospective members should note that detailed rate information, specific loan terms, and eligibility requirements are not published on the website—these must be obtained directly through contact. The current ATM system maintenance suggests potential service interruptions. As with all financial institutions, credit union membership may have specific eligibility requirements that are not disclosed on the homepage. The website lacks comprehensive information about membership fees, minimum deposits, or product-specific terms.
services: Fraud protection and card replacement services
Member account support via phone and fax
pros: Credit union structure typically offers better rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Comprehensive product offerings including checking, savings, credit cards, auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans
Established routing number and regulatory compliance indicates legitimacy and stability
Multiple contact methods available (phone, fax, email) for member support
Published routing number 301281968 enables direct deposits and ACH transfers
Physically located in St. Joseph, MO with accessible main office and mailing address
Member-centric messaging suggests long-term member retention focus
cons: ATM network currently down with no specified restoration date, limiting cash access
Website lacks published interest rates, making rate comparisons impossible without contacting the institution
No information about membership eligibility requirements or potential membership fees
Limited online presence—website is basic and lacks details about loan terms, fees, or minimum deposit requirements
No information about online banking features, mobile apps, or digital service capabilities
best_for: St. Joseph, Missouri residents seeking comprehensive banking services from a local, member-owned institution
Consumers prioritizing credit union membership benefits and potentially lower fees/better rates
Members seeking traditional full-service banking with mortgages, auto loans, and personal loans
Individuals with established community ties seeking member-owned financial institution
diagnosis: Postal & Community Credit Union is best for St. Joseph area residents and established members seeking traditional credit union banking with comprehensive loan products. The main caveat is that current ATM maintenance, lack of published rates, and limited online information require direct contact to evaluate specific terms and service capabilities.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
